bonuses.leadin: A twisted sculpture of metal situated at the top of this building is nicknamed Medusa. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A twisted sculpture of metal situated at the top of this building is nicknamed Medusa. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this building in Prague, often nicknamed for Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers due to its slant.

This Czech architect is one of two architects who collaborated on designing the Dancing House. This architect was one of five Czech architects commissioned to design Shanghai's “Czech Quarter”, as well as designing several neighbourhoods in the Petřiny district of Prague.

After Jean Nouvel turned down the job, this Canadian-American architect collaborated with Milunić in designing The Dancing House. This architect also designed Bilbao’s Guggenheim Museum and Seattle’s Pop Culture Museum.
parts_sanitized: Name this building in Prague, often nicknamed for Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers due to its slant.

After Jean Nouvel turned down the job, this Canadian-American architect collaborated with Milunic in designing The Dancing House. This architect also designed Bilbao's Guggenheim Museum and Seattle's Pop Culture Museum.
answers_sanitized: Frank Gehry [or Frank Owen Gehry; accept Frank Goldberg]

bonuses.leadin: In 2017, Rome mayor Virginia Raggi prevented three triangular towers designed by this architect from being included in the park next to football club AS (“ah-ess”) Roma’s planned new stadium. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Polish-American architect whose Jewish Museum in Berlin has several empty “voids” lacking air conditioning, one of which contains thousands of iron cutouts resembling human faces.
David Childs of Skidmore, Owings, and Merrill discarded much of Libeskind’s preliminary design for this New York skyscraper built as a spiritual replacement to the Twin Towers destroyed on 9/11.

Libeskind’s “Crystal” was a controversial addition to a museum in this country. Moshe Safdie (“mo-SHEH SAHF-dee”) built a modular “Habitat” for a World’s Fair in this country, the home country of the architect of the Dancing House.
answers: One World Trade Center [or One WTC; or Freedom Tower; prompt on World Trade Center or WTC]
Canada (The architect of the Dancing House is Frank Gehry, and Habitat 67 was for Montreal’s Expo.)
